question: Is the background clearly recognizable as the specific ink wash mountain painting provided in the input?
Is the added object clearly recognizable as a quadcopter drone, retaining its core structure (central body and four arms) despite the stylization?
Is the drone positioned logically within the scene (e.g., flying in the sky or mist) without conflicting with foreground elements like trees or rocks?
Is the drone rendered using a monochromatic ink palette (blacks, grays, sepias) consistent with the background, completely avoiding the bright white or blue colors of the original photo?
Does the drone exhibit traditional brushstroke characteristics (e.g., varying line weights, ink wash diffusion) rather than appearing as a photorealistic or 3D-rendered object?
Does the drone integration utilize negative space (liu bai) or dry brush effects (fei bai) to blend naturally with the misty atmosphere, avoiding hard digital edges?
Is the drone scaled plausibly relative to the mountains, appearing as a distant object rather than dwarfing the landscape features?
Is the paper/canvas texture consistent across the entire image, ensuring the drone looks painted on the same surface as the background?
Is the image free from obvious compositing artifacts such as haloing, pixelation, or sharp cut-out borders around the drone?
Is the drone free from severe structural distortions, such as melted arms, disconnected rotors, or asymmetrical appendages?
